Job Title: Senior or Associate Geospatial Analyst - Perth
Ad Text:
Shape the Future with CDM Smith Australia – Senior or Associate Geospatial Analyst

Are you ready to leave your mark on the world and build a legacy for generations to come?

At CDM Smith Australia, we believe meaningful careers start with connection. We aren’t simply hiring, we’re partnering with passionate professionals who want to make a real difference. Your expertise, vision and values are what matter most to us. If you’re looking for more than just a job, join us to shape the future of environmental services across Western Australia and beyond.

About You

Between 7 and 10 years of GIS experience, preferably in a consulting environment (Senior Level).

10+ years of GIS experience, preferably in a consulting environment (Associate Level). 

Tertiary qualifications in GIS, Environmental Science, or equivalent.

Experience leading a GIS team in a dynamic project focused environment including balancing team workload, supporting staff development, tracking performance against key metrics, connecting and collaborating with a wide variety of stakeholders, and helping lead strategic improvements.

Extensive knowledge of and experience with Esri GIS software (e.g. ArcMap, ArcGIS Pro, Portal, AGOL etc).

Python experience (desirable) and FME experience (desirable)

Creative problem-solving skills to work autonomously, and as part of a team, and a genuine interest in innovation, continual learning and improvement.

Experience, or the ability, to take ownership of a work front and proactively collaborate with stakeholders to deliver outcomes and improve processes in a dynamic environment. 

Have robust analytical skills, with experience in the management, manipulation, analysis and spatial presentation of data.

Solid organisational and time management skills.

Outstanding communication skills, and confidence in building positive relationships.

Ability to grasp new concepts quickly, be adaptable and flexible, and contribute individually and within a team environment to the success of a project.

Full legal working rights within Australia.

Why CDM Smith Australia?

Shape the Future with Us in Western Australia
We’re tackling Australia’s most pressing environmental, water, and digital challenges head-on. Here, your work will have genuine impact—not only for our clients, but for communities and the planet itself. You’ll be empowered to develop innovative solutions, drive technical excellence, and build a sustainable future for Western Australia. With us, your ideas and energy will help set the agenda for the region’s growth and environmental progress, steering Western Australia into a brighter, greener tomorrow.

About the Role
We are seeking talented Senior or Associate GIS Analyst to lead and deliver GIS analysis and spatial solutions across diverse environmental and engineering projects. Based in our Perth office or client sites, you’ll work closely with project teams to scope and implement integrated spatial solutions that enhance project outcomes.

In this role, you’ll also play a key part in driving innovation and improving spatial services, collaborating with our Technical Practice Leader – Digital to identify opportunities for smarter delivery. This is your chance to combine technical expertise with creativity, shaping solutions that make a real difference.

As part of the CDM Smith Team you have

Flexibility: Design your work-life balance with genuine flexible working options.

Support: 12 weeks paid parental leave, 2-week secondary carer leave, health & wellness programs, and annual flu vax.

Recognition: Celebrate your achievements through our employee recognition program.

Growth: Unlimited access to learning platforms, mentoring from industry leaders, and global mobility options.

Rewards: Referral bonuses, employee discounts, and benefits programs.

Belonging: Be part of a global, employee-owned consulting firm committed to diversity, inclusion and a positive workplace culture.

Join Us – Make Your Mark

Ready to be part of a team where your insight, expertise and ideas truly matter? Where client relationships are built on trust, and solutions are shaped by passion and knowledge? At CDM Smith Australia, we’re growing because we invest in our people and our clients by delivering fit-for-purpose, sustainable answers for industry’s toughest problems.

We welcome applicants from all backgrounds. Diversity, technical ability, integrity and innovation are the hallmarks of our team. If you want to be heard, valued and inspired, let's shape your NOW and your NEXT, together.

Let’s get started: For more information or a confidential chat, reach out to Senior Human Resources Business Partner – au-recruit@cdmsmith.com

At CDM Smith, we Listen, Think, Deliver.

We can't wait to hear your story.

Job Title: Senior Functional Consultant - Microsoft Business Central
Ad Text:

Eden Ritchie Recruitment are seeking a Data Analyst and a Digital Technology Officer to support key data and digital initiatives that enhance service delivery, improve decision-making, and drive operational efficiency across the organisation.
 
Senior Data Analyst
In this role, you will transform complex data into actionable insights to support strategic decision-making. You will work across modern data platforms, develop reporting solutions, and contribute to data-driven improvements across the organisation.

Key responsibilities:

Develop and maintain dashboards and reports (Power BI and similar tools)
Design and manage data pipelines (ETL/ELT processes)
Work with large datasets using SQL and cloud platforms (e.g. Azure, GCP, Snowflake)
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand reporting and analytics requirements
Provide insights to support operational and strategic outcomes
Contribute to data governance and data quality initiatives

About you:

Strong SQL and data analysis capability
Experience with Power BI and modern data platforms
Knowledge of ETL/data integration processes
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Ability to work in a collaborative, fast-paced environment
 
Senior Digital Technology Officer
This role focuses on delivering and supporting digital solutions using the Microsoft Power Platform. You will work closely with stakeholders to design, build, and maintain low-code solutions that enhance business processes and user experience.

Experience with Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI)

Strong communication and documentation skills
 
If you are a data-driven problem solver or a digitally savvy professional who enjoys turning business needs into practical solutions, this is your opportunity to make a real impact. Apply now to be part of a collaborative environment where your expertise in data, reporting, and digital technologies will help drive meaningful outcomes.
 
For more information or to discuss the role, please contact Bree Woods on bree@edenritchie.com.au or 07 3230 0038

We’ve shared the client’s name to help you assess your suitability for the role. Please DO NOT contact them directly.

About the role

We are seeking an experienced Programme Manager to lead the delivery of a complex, enterprise‑wide transformation programme on a fixed‑term contract until 30 September 2027.

This programme includes migrating from a legacy IBM ERP system to SAP S/4HANA, while integrating multiple business‑critical platforms including Salesforce, CPFR/PSI forecasting solutions, rebate and pricing systems, 3PL, EDI and BI tools, ensuring end‑to‑end delivery, strong governance and sustainable business adoption.

You will be accountable for the full programme delivery, covering IT implementation, data migration, business process transformation, change management and post go‑live stabilisation. You will act as the single point of accountability across the business, IT and external partners.

 

Key responsibilities include:

Owning overall programme governance, scope, budget, timelines and risk management
Managing multiple workstreams, including SAP, Salesforce, planning/CPFR, pricing, rebate management, data migration, BI and change management
Leading systems integrator partners, data migration teams, internal IT teams, business working groups and key stakeholders
Ensuring alignment between business objectives, data readiness and system implementation
Engaging and influencing senior stakeholders to maintain alignment and commitment

 

Project work

SAP S/4HANA Transformation

Lead SAP S/4HANA implementation (Greenfield)
Oversee core modules including SD, MM and FI/CO

Salesforce & Peripheral Systems

Oversee Salesforce implementation for Spare Parts Ordering and Warranty Claims
Ensure seamless integration between SAP and Salesforce via APIs and middleware, including 3PL interfaces and EDI
Manage CPFR / PSI forecasting, customer base selling price and rebate solutions, and their integration with ERP

Integration & External Interfaces

Oversee 3PL integrations, including inbound and outbound logistics, ASN, goods receipt and invoicing

BI & Analytics

Lead the migration from QlikView to Power BI
Deliver end‑to‑end business‑critical Power BI reporting, leveraging data from SAP S/4HANA, Salesforce, regional data lakehouse and external platforms

 

About you

You have 10+ years’ experience leading large‑scale, complex IT and business transformation programmes, demonstrating strong leadership and sound decision‑making, with the confidence to challenge stakeholders on data ownership and data quality.

In addition, you will have:

Formal training in programme / project management frameworks (e.g. PMI, PMP or PRINCE2 Practitioner)
SAP S/4HANA certification preferred but not mandatory
Experience in consumer goods, manufacturing, distribution or retail
Experience working in regional or global matrix organisations
Proven track record as a Programme Manager or Senior Project Manager delivering end‑to‑end programmes across ERP (preferably SAP S/4HANA) and CRM (preferably Salesforce)
Demonstrated experience managing multiple parallel workstreams and complex dependencies
Strong background in governance, financial control, risk and benefits management
Proven ownership of large‑scale ERP migrations

 

Why join us?

Lead a visible, business‑critical programme for a reputable organisation
Work closely with senior leaders and key decision‑makers
Opportunity to make a tangible impact within a defined timeframe
Competitive remuneration and staff benefits, including hybrid working options
A supportive and collaborative company culture

 

Next steps

If you’re a results‑driven Programme Manager ready to lead with impact, apply now - we’d love to hear from you.

As a Senior Functional Consultant, you’ll manage multiple ERP implementation projects, contribute to solution design, and oversee junior consultants within project teams. This is a dynamic role with scope to grow into a senior leadership position.

Own critical mainframe data pipelines, lead strategy, and drive analytics uplift for a major federal agency.



Your new company
You'll be joining a large federal government agency at the forefront of major national service delivery. The organisation is investing heavily in modernising its data and technology capabilities, with a strong focus on high‑performance data engineering, advanced analytics, and the uplift of mission‑critical platforms.


Your new role
As the Lead Mainframe Data Engineer, you will design, build, and optimise mainframe data pipelines supporting large‑scale data warehouse and analytics environments. You'll work with complex datasets, develop SAS and JCL solutions, improve data quality and integrity, and provide technical leadership across data engineering standards, modelling, governance, and integration. You'll also mentor junior engineers and drive continuous improvement initiatives across the data function.


Key duties and responsibilities
Duties may include some or all of the following:

Develop, test, and maintain SAS programs for data extraction, transformation, and reporting.
Collaborate with business analysts and subject-matter experts to translate requirements into technical solutions.
Optimise mainframe data pipelines for performance and efficiency.
Ensure compliance with data governance, privacy, and security requirements.
Produce clear documentation for code, processes, and data workflows.
Provide technical advice and mentoring to junior engineers.
Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives in data analytics and reporting.
Ability to work with large, complex datasets and perform advanced data manipulation.
Analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
Ability to work independently and manage competing priorities
Technical skills

SAS Base
SAS Macro
SAS/SQL
Job Control Language (JCL)
IBM Mainframe
DB2 Knowledge
Mainframe Production Support
Control-M
SCLM (IBM Change Control Utility)
Strong SQL skills and experience with relational databases.
Demonstrated experience in SAS development within a data analytics or reporting environment.
Prior experience within government or similar large-scale organisation preferred







What you'll need to succeed
You will bring strong SAS Base/Macro/SQL expertise, deep experience across IBM mainframe environments (JCL, DB2, Control‑M, SCLM), and the ability to work with large datasets in complex enterprise settings. You'll demonstrate senior‑level capability in data engineering, data management, modelling, BI, quality assurance, and integration. Strong analytical thinking, documentation skills, stakeholder engagement, and the ability to work autonomously are essential. Baseline clearance is required.


What you'll get in return
A 12‑month contract with up to 24‑month extension options, competitive daily rates, and the chance to contribute to high impact national programs. You'll work in a mature data environment with modernisation underway, offering both stability and meaningful technical influence. Positions are available in Canberra or Adelaide, with standard 37.5‑hour work weeks.


What you need to do now
If you're interested in this role, please email your up to date CV to me at george.pinkerton@hays.com.au
If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career.

Do you want to build a finance function that powers national growth, not maintain spreadsheets for an organisation that's already figured it out?

Contour has proven its product-market fit. What began as a scrappy startup is now a high-growth scale-up ranked on the AFR Fast 100 four years in a row (#8 in 2025), and our finance function will be central to how we grow, acquire, and scale in 2026 and beyond.

We're seeking a Financial Controller who is a builder, not just a bean counter. This role offers end-to-end ownership of the numbers that drive our organisation: from month-end close to M&A readiness, from cashflow stewardship to entity integration. You won't just be reporting on the past; you will be building the financial infrastructure that makes confident growth possible.

Your mandate: control, scale, and partner.







Who We Are

At Contour, we’re reshaping education, one student at a time. 

Since 2020, over 10,000+ students have trusted Contour to guide their academic journey, and we’ve grown to over 300+ team members. We’ve produced hundreds of 99+ ATARs, tutored the World-Record UCAT scorer, and placed more students into medical school than any school in Australia. 

Our growth is a testament to the simple philosophy: Put the student first, always. Everything we design, from our world-class tutor-training programs to our purpose-built campuses, exists to help students master content faster, perform with confidence, and even rekindle the joy of learning. 

Contour is a group of three brands united by one goal: to give every student the best chance to achieve their full potential:

Contour Education: Reshaping how students master their high school subjects with small-group tutoring, structured resources, and 1-on-1 support across Maths, Science, and English.
Contour Medprep: Reshaping medical preparation with a proven, step-by-step pathway through the UCAT, interviews, and admissions, led by a team of 100+ medical students. 
Contour TestPrep: Reshaping test preparation, starting with the selective entry exam, through rigorous tutoring, mock exams, and data-driven feedback to give students a decisive advantage. 

This is Contour. Built by students, for students.




What You Will Do

Reporting to the CFO, you will serve as the operational anchor of Contour's finance function, with direct management of the Group Payroll Manager, Finance Analyst, and Junior Accountant.

Financial Reporting & Control

Own end-to-end month-end and year-end close processes across all entities, delivering financial statements within agreed SLAs.
Prepare executive reporting packs with clear variance commentary, forward-looking narrative, and decision-grade insight.
Lead audit preparation and maintain ongoing audit readiness, including chart of accounts, intercompany reconciliation, and group elimination entries.
Maintain strong internal controls and documentation standards across a multi-campus, multi-entity structure.

Budgeting, Forecasting & Financial Planning

Lead the annual budgeting process in partnership with department leaders and the CFO.
Maintain rolling 12-month forecasts with scenario modelling, including upside and downside cases for growth scenarios.
Deliver monthly variance analysis with forward-looking risk and opportunity identification.
Instil financial discipline and budget accountability across the organisation.

Cashflow & Working Capital Management

Maintain accurate short- and medium-term cashflow forecasts and monitor revenue, margin, and cost performance across campuses and entities.
Improve debtor collection cycles and optimise working capital efficiency.
Manage banking relationships at an operational level, including facility compliance and covenant reporting.
Escalate material financial risks to the CFO in a timely, structured, and solutions-oriented manner.

Multi-Entity Growth & Integration

Provide financial analysis and modelling support for new entity establishment, interstate expansion, and structural growth initiatives.
Lead the financial integration of new entities: chart of accounts alignment, system migration, reporting integration, and control implementation.
Build financial models for scenario evaluation, performance tracking, and growth planning.
Establish day-one finance readiness plans for newly established or restructured entities.
Navigate ambiguity: make sound judgement calls on financial treatment and process design where precedent does not exist.

Systems, Compliance & Process Optimisation

Strengthen finance systems, automation, and reporting infrastructure to support multi-entity, multi-campus operations.
Document and implement a scalable Finance Operations Manual.
Ensure compliance with AASB accounting standards, tax obligations (income tax, GST, FBT, payroll tax), and all statutory requirements.
Evaluate and recommend system investments (ERP, consolidation tools, BI) as the organisation scales.
Lead budgeting, forecasting, reporting and long-term financial strategy
Provide commercial insights that shape decision-making across the business
Manage cash flow, compliance, audit, tax, and financial risk across jurisdictions
Build a best-in-class finance function across Payroll, Billing, Accounts and FP&A
Introduce scalable financial systems, automation and performance dashboards
Partner with the executive team on profitability, pricing and capital strategy
Oversee annual audit, cross-functional financial projects and systems upgrades
Lead and develop a high-performing team through coaching and accountability
Ensure accurate, timely and transparent financial reporting
Drive financial alignment across People, Academic, Operations and Tech teams




What You WIll Bring
Technical Credentials: CA or CPA qualified (or equivalent), with 7+ years in financial control, management accounting, or senior finance roles, including at least 2 years at controller or equivalent level.
Multi-Entity Experience: Demonstrated involvement in entity integration, multi-site consolidation, or standing up finance operations in new or restructured entities.
Scale-Up Background: Track record in high-growth, multi-site, or service-sector organisations. Education sector experience is a bonus, not a requirement.
Financial Modelling Capability: Strong budgeting, forecasting, and scenario analysis skills, with the ability to translate numbers into operational insight.
Systems Orientation: Demonstrated experience improving finance systems, controls, and reporting infrastructure.
Execution Under Ambiguity: Comfortable building structure where it does not yet exist, and making sound judgement calls when perfect information is unavailable.
High Ownership Mindset: Highly structured and process-oriented, with strong upward reporting discipline and the instinct to close loops without being chased.
Mission-Alignment: Genuinely motivated by contributing to meaningful educational impact.




Why You Will Love It
Impact & Ownership: You will own the finance function below CFO level, with real influence over the financial decisions that shape Contour's national growth trajectory.
Complexity Without Corporate Bureaucracy: Multi-entity, multi-campus, and actively expanding nationally, you'll work across the full spectrum of financial complexity without the red tape of a large corporate.
A Seat at the Table: You will partner directly with the CFO and executive leadership team, translating financial performance into strategic decisions that matter.
Growth Potential: Opportunity for career development as Contour scales into a national education group, with a finance function that grows with it.
Flexible Environment: Hybrid-friendly, with on-site presence at our Melbourne campuses to connect with the team and operational leaders.
Competitive Package: Attractive salary commensurate with experience, plus super, leave entitlements, EAP access, and lifestyle perks.

Ready to build a finance function that sustains something that lasts? If you want to bring financial precision and commercial rigour to a mission that genuinely matters, we want to hear from you.


How to Apply

Ready to make a real impact? Here is our application process:

Submit your application.
Shortlisted candidates will complete a video interview and a case-based assessment.
Suitable candidates will then be invited for a panel interview.

Ventia
Position: Senior Integration Analyst - Evolve Program
Location: North Sydney Office, Hybrid working (3 days from office per week)
Monday - Friday usual business hours

Primary Responsibilities

The Senior Integration Analyst is accountable for the end‑to‑end quality, design, and governance of
integration solutions across SAP and non‑SAP systems within the Evolve Program. This includes
ownership of integration design outcomes, ensuring alignment with enterprise architecture, and driving
adoption of modern, API‑led, and event‑driven integration patterns.

The role acts as a senior integration SME, collaborating closely with system integrators (SIs) and internal
teams to drive effective and sustainable integration solutions that adhere to SAP Clean Core principles and
best‑practice integration standards.

Key responsibilities include leading integration design reviews, ensuring event‑driven and API‑based
architectures are applied effectively, collaborating with SI resources, and proactively identifying risks and
improvements across hybrid and cloud‑native integration landscapes.

Experience and Qualifications
Essential
* Over 7 years of experience in enterprise integration across SAP and non‑SAP platforms.
* Proven expertise in SAP BTP Integration Suite and Advanced Event Mesh, including hands‑on
experience designing event‑driven architectures.

Field Service Management ).

Job Title: Senior Manager Client Advisory - Visa Consulting & Analytics
Ad Text:
Company Description


Visa is a world leader in payments technology, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories, dedicated to uplifting everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid.

At Visa, you'll have the opportunity to create impact at scale — tackling meaningful challenges, growing your skills and seeing your contributions impact lives around the world. Join Visa and do work that matters — to you, to your community, and to the world.

Progress starts with you.

Job Description


Visa Consulting and Analytics (VCA) is the consulting arm of Visa, and drives tangible, impactful results for clients. Drawing on our expertise in consulting, data analytics, technology, payments and economics, VCA solves the most strategic problems for our clients. VCA's core client segments include issuers, acquirers, merchants, fintechs, payment enablers and governments.

The team provides a comprehensive range of consulting services and solutions to our clients across Australia, New Zealand and the Pacific Islands that address unique challenges in areas such as strategic growth, profitability, fraud risk management and digital strategy.        

Senior Manager Client Advisory role

VCA is looking for a Senior Manager Client Advisory to work alongside the Account Executives in Sydney, Australia, to identify, sell in and take ownership of a programme of consulting engagements with key clients, that supports the growth of their business, as well as Visa’s.

The Senior Manager Client Advisory will be responsible for identifying new business development opportunities, and alongside our delivery team, designing and overseeing the successful delivery of high-quality client engagements. This will involve working closely with the account leads and senior client stakeholders to gain a deep understanding of our clients’ business and outlining a programme of advisory work intended to support them in achieving their business objectives. The Senior Manager Client Advisory will be required to oversee a programme of work, identifying the client challenges, outlining a broad programme of support including specific advisory assignments, designing consulting approaches for each, liaising with VCA’s Practice teams to write the proposals, providing input to delivery, ensuring high quality output.

The areas where Visa provides support to clients is wide and varied and could include industry landscape evaluations to inform payments strategies, proposition strategy and design, portfolio optimisation, leveraging Visa’s data set to inform a broad range of data-related initiatives, process/operations optimisation, supporting in the management fraud & scams to name a few.

The full range of responsibilities are detailed below:

Responsibilities

Work in close partnership with Account Executives to develop a deep understanding of client needs and challenges, participate in account planning and identify a programme of advisory opportunities that will support the growth of our clients’ business.
Prepare client project proposals for each advisory opportunity, engaging with clients to clearly define objectives, working with VCA’s Practice team to agree scope, approach and deliverables, define roles and responsibilities and delivery timelines.
Oversee project setup including contracting documentation, stakeholder engagement, interview guides and client data requirements.
Manage execution of one or more project engagements, building client relationships, utilising governance frameworks and mitigating / resolving complex problems that emerge.
Design and deliver compelling presentations to illustrate findings, ideas and recommendations and more broadly at times support implementation at the client onsite.

More generally:

Identify opportunities for other Visa functions including marketing, product, client services.
Continuously build knowledge, surface new insights, and produce thought leadership pieces that contribute to practice development and global best practice.

This is a hybrid position. Expectation of days in office will be confirmed by your hiring manager.

Qualifications


Having already accumulated a variety of experience, you will be familiar with the payments industry and keen to work alongside an experienced team in gaining a deeper understanding. Specific skills and experience you will bring are:

8+ years of experience in consulting, corporate strategy, or business management with a Bachelor’s Degree.
Exceptional interpersonal and communication skills, adept at building strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders. Ability to articulate complex ideas clearly and effectively, both verbally and in writing.
Innovative thinking, familiar with a range of consulting approaches that could be leveraged to solve client challenges, to deciphering complex problems and developing practical solutions.
Highly organised with experience in simultaneously managing multiple projects, with varying levels of complexity.
Strong team orientation with a collaborative, diplomatic, and flexible style, able to work effectively in a matrixed organization.
Excellent attention to detail, ensuring the highest level of quality/rigour in project deliverables.
Self-motivated, work well independently, managing your own time and resources effectively. Able to handle pressure and setbacks and adapt to change quickly and effectively.
Strong commercial acumen, with experience in working on business/value cases that prove ROI.

In addition, you will ideally bring the following:

Experience in payments or retail banking industry.
Experience in developing strategy (for products or business units).
Experience in customer experience design (using human centred design / design thinking).

Additional Information


Visa is an EEO Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability or protected veteran status. Visa will also consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with EEOC guidelines and applicable local law.

Findex is seeking a commercial and driven FP&A Manager to join our Finance team supporting our Professional Services and Advice business. This role reports to the Senior FP&A Manager and focuses on providing high quality financial insights for our Audit, Advisory, and Wealth divisions. You will act as a key business partner, identifying trends and supporting strategic decision making to help our business grow. This position is ideal for a finance professional who enjoys building relationships and translating complex data into actionable business strategies.

Your Role, Your Contribution

As the FP&A Manager – Professional Services & Advice, you are responsible for the financial planning and strategic analysis of your assigned divisions. Your key responsibilities include:

Delivering management reports that highlight performance drivers, operational targets, and strategic risks while providing actionable recommendations.
Leading the annual budgeting process and monthly forecasting to ensure accurate submissions and identify risks to performance.
Acting as a trusted advisor to divisional leadership by providing financial guidance on business cases and cost optimisation initiatives.
Contributing to the development of workforce plans for the Audit, Advisory, and Wealth service lines.
Developing financial models and analytical tools to improve decision support and driving the adoption of AI models within the finance function.
Building strong, collaborative relationships across the organisation to promote financial literacy and accountability.

The Benefits of Joining Us

Dynamic work environment, work from home and office – great flexibility
Support for your career growth through exposure to senior leadership and complex commercial projects.
Work within a dedicated Finance team that prioritises sharing ideas and continuous improvement.
Use modern visualisation tools and AI models to influence and drive business strategy.
A role that encourages a self-managed approach, allowing you to take ownership of financial insights.

What You Bring to the Role

To be successful in this position, you will possess a blend of technical expertise and strong interpersonal skills. Requirements include:

A tertiary degree in Business or Commerce and a post-graduate qualification such as CA or CPA.
Proven experience in a senior FP&A or commercial finance role, specifically in planning and performance reporting.
Strong Microsoft Excel skills including financial modelling, with exposure to visualisation tools like Power BI or Tableau preferred.
Proven ability to lead and influence across functional areas and act as a strategic advisor to senior leadership.
High attention to detail combined with a proactive, self-directed approach to solving complex problems.

If this role sounds like a good fit - Apply now!  

 

Our Impact: Why Findex?    

At Findex, we help clients manage, grow, and protect their financial future with confidence. Since 1987, we’ve become a leading Australasian provider of integrated accounting, advisory, and wealth solutions, delivered through our “Wealth Accountant” and Family Office models. With 2,500 professionals across 100+ offices, we combine local expertise with global reach and remain committed to supporting clients and communities in creating long-term financial security.  

For the Community - The Findex Community Fund (FCF) is dedicated to giving back to the communities we serve. Since 2018, we have donated over $4.3 million to more than 800 charities across Australia and New Zealand. Collectively, our team has dedicated over 9,000 volunteer hours, making a tangible impact in their communities through personal engagement.  

For You - At Findex, we focus on developing our people first. With structured training and development opportunities, we allow our people to grow their career in a way that sets them up for success. We actively support our people by giving them extra support to external studies that is relevant to their role.    

For our Customers - Together, Findex champion the success of over 75,000 Australasian businesses, helping them grow with expert advice, fresh thinking, and top-tier professional services. Here, you’re empowered to work in a way that brings out your best — supporting your growth, your team, and the clients who trust us to make a real difference    

For Diversity & Inclusion - Findex is an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and encourage applications from candidates with a variety of strengths, backgrounds and experiences.

Product Lead - Integration
 
Role Purpose The Product Lead - Integration oversees the strategic execution and operational delivery of enterprise integration platforms. This role is accountable for all design, delivery, and support within complex SAP landscapes, leveraging SAP Integration Suite, PI/PO, CPI, and MuleSoft to ensure seamless connectivity and digital transformation.
 
Key Accountabilities

Strategy & Vision: Define integration patterns and API-led connectivity aligned with enterprise architecture; maintain a comprehensive product roadmap and engage stakeholders to incorporate feedback into development.
Leadership: Foster a collaborative environment across digital functions and manage external product vendors to ensure cohesive delivery.
Delivery & Operations: Manage the full integration lifecycle, including design, deployment, and 24/7 monitoring; prioritize the product backlog and ensure robust error handling and compliance with enterprise standards.
Software Currency: Maintain supportability and version control for all integration landscapes; develop annual initiatives for patching and platform maintenance.
Governance: Establish and enforce vendor governance frameworks and manage service provider activities within the integration landscape.
Skills & Experience

Expertise: 10+ years in integration toolsets with 5+ years in a technical leadership capacity, specifically within large-scale, SAP-centric FMCG environments.
Technical Proficiency: Deep knowledge of SAP Integration Suite, CPI, PI/PO, MuleSoft, and SAP data models (IDocs, BAPIs, oDATA).
Qualifications: ITIL Foundations and SAP Integration Suite/CPI certifications required; Agile certification preferred.
Communication: Proven ability to influence senior stakeholders and manage cross-functional teams in high-availability, mission-critical settings.

Provide forward thinking leadership in research analytics, leveraging emerging technologies and foster strategic partnerships with external research and ranking bodies

About the position

Research Performance plays a critical role in advancing La Trobe University’s research and innovation impact, global reputation and compliance outcomes. The team leads the development, analysis and communication of research and innovation data for both internal and external stakeholders, ensuring information is accurate, accessible and strategically aligned. Working in close collaboration with Schools, central portfolios, and external bodies including research funders, data providers and ranking agencies the team drives continuous improvement in how research performance is measured, communicated and used to inform strategic decisions.

The Senior Manager, Research Performance provides high level advice to the Deputy Vice-Chancellor (Research and Industry Engagement), academic leaders and professional staff on research performance and its broader context. The role is responsible for delivering insightful analysis to support the implementation of the University’s research and innovation strategy, as well as leading the development and execution of the institutional ranking’s strategy.

Some of your duties will include:

Lead the Research Performance team to deliver high-quality analysis, reporting and continuous improvement in research and innovation performance.
Provide strategic advice to senior leadership on research performance, rankings and evaluation frameworks.
Oversee accurate and timely submissions to government and ranking bodies to support the University’s reputation and compliance.
Support the implementation and monitoring of the University’s research and innovation strategy.
Collaborate with schools, portfolios and central teams to ensure performance insights inform academic and business planning.
Drive the development and maintenance of research systems in partnership with key stakeholders.
Stay ahead of emerging trends in research analytics, including AI/ML, and maintain relationships with key external bodies.
Contribute to leadership across the research office and lead major strategic projects as required.

Skills & Experience:

Postgraduate qualifications and/or extensive experience in strategic policy and organisational leadership.
Strong understanding of higher education and research, including global trends, ranking systems and quality assessment.
Proven ability to lead and implement strategy in complex environments working collaboratively with senior stakeholders.
Skilled in using research performance data to inform decisions.
Strong leadership, negotiation and stakeholder engagement skills.
Excellent communication and representation skills, with experience engaging high level stakeholders.
Demonstrated success in managing complex projects, stakeholder strategies and working across organisational boundaries.
Sound financial, budgeting and change management experience, including technology enabled initiatives.

Job Title: Head of Clinical Governance, Best Practice and Research
Ad Text:
About the Organisation

Feros Care is a purpose-driven organisation committed to empowering people to live their best life. With a strong presence in the aged care and community services sector, the organisation is recognised for delivering high-quality, innovative, and person-centred care that truly makes a difference.

Guided by bold values and a culture of innovation, collaboration, and compassion, Feros Care is redefining what exceptional care looks like. Their people are passionate, forward-thinking, and united by a shared commitment to continuous improvement, meaningful impact, and creating better outcomes for the communities they serve.

About the Position

As the Head of Clinical Governance, Best Practice and Research, you will play a critical leadership role in shaping clinical excellence across the organisation. Reporting to the CEO, you will lead governance frameworks, research initiatives, and evidence-based models of care to ensure services are safe, compliant, and continuously improving.

This is a strategic and highly influential role where you will partner with senior leaders, drive innovation in care delivery, and embed a culture of learning and quality improvement. You’ll have the opportunity to elevate organisational capability, strengthen research partnerships, and position the organisation as a leader in best practice and clinical innovation.

About the Person

You are an experienced and values-driven leader with a strong background in clinical governance, quality systems, and research within aged care, health, or community services. With exceptional leadership and communication skills, you are comfortable influencing at executive and board level, translating complex data into meaningful insights, and inspiring high-performing teams. You are forward-thinking, collaborative, and motivated by the opportunity to shape the future of care through innovation, research, and impact.

To submit your application, please click Apply Now and include your resume and cover letter by Friday 10th April 2026 or contact Tim Turner on 0414 548 052 or t.turner@humero.com.au for a confidential discussion.

The role supports budgeting, forecasting, and performance reporting, while also guiding project funding decisions through business case evaluation, financial modelling, and benefits tracking. Use advanced data analysis to generate insights from complex datasets
Challenge assumptions and improve forecast modelling standards

Proven ability to deliver high-quality, insightful analysis to drive business performance

Job Title: Data Steward / Master Data Governance Officer / Information & Metadata Analyst
Ad Text:
WFH Hybrid – 2 days in office at present
Immediate Start
$800 to $896 per day
Contract initially till end of June 2026 with strong prospect of renewal
High profile NSW Government program
Brand-new project
An excellent opportunity is available for an experienced Data Steward / Data Governance & Metadata Analyst / Data Custodian to join a NSW Government agency and play a key role in strengthening data governance, metadata management, data security, and data quality practices across a major program of work.

We are seeking a well-rounded professional with strong communication skills and a solid understanding of data structures, data interconnections, metadata, data catalogues, data glossaries, and data governance frameworks. This role will be instrumental in helping define, document and govern how data is understood, used, secured and managed across the organisation.

This is a great opportunity for someone who can work closely with stakeholders to obtain permissions, clarify appropriate data usage, and support the development of data policies, security procedures, data standards and governance artefacts.

Experience with the Aristotle metadata tool will be highly regarded, though it is not essential.

Essential skills and experience:

Demonstrated experience in a Data Stewardship, Data Governance Analyst, Metadata Analyst, Information Governance Analyst or similar role
Strong understanding of data governance, metadata, data cataloguing, data glossary development and data management principles
Experience writing and maintaining data definitions, business glossaries, data catalogues, reference materials, policies or governance documentation
Strong understanding of data structures, data relationships, data flows and data interconnections
Experience working with stakeholders to clarify data meaning, usage, ownership, permissions and controls
Ability to develop or support policy, procedure and security documentation related to data use and governance
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Strong stakeholder engagement skills with the ability to work across business and technical teams
High attention to detail and strong documentation capability
Highly desirable:

Experience in NSW Government or broader government environments
Experience with a metadata management tool such as Aristotle
Exposure to data security, information governance, data policy, master data, reference data or data quality frameworks
Understanding of enterprise data environments and governance best practice
Key responsibilities:

Develop, maintain and enhance the organisation’s data glossary, data catalogue and data reference documentation
Support and implement data governance, metadata management and data stewardship practices
Work with stakeholders to identify, define and document critical data elements, business terms, data definitions and reference data
Engage with business and technical stakeholders to seek permissions, obtain clarifications, and support compliant and appropriate use of data
Assist in developing and documenting data policies, data standards, security procedures and governance controls around the use of data
Support understanding of data lineage, data structures, data relationships and data interconnections across systems
Help improve consistency, quality, transparency and governance of enterprise data assets
Contribute to the uplift of data security, data ownership and controlled access practices
Support governance forums, working groups and stakeholder discussions relating to metadata, policy and data usage
If you are a strong Data Steward / Metadata Analyst / Data Governance Analyst with experience in data glossary, data catalogue, metadata, data governance, policy writing, and stakeholder engagement, we would love to hear from you.

Best method to apply is using the application button on this advert. We can be contacted on (02) 9687 1025 for a confidential discussion but please ensure the resume has been sent.
    
Please ensure all documents are sent in Microsoft word format.

We’ve been proudly serving Australian communities since 1914 and have grown to become a top 30-listed company on the ASX with a portfolio of iconic homegrown brands. At the heart of our continued success is over 115,000 team members working across the country, all contributing to bringing our customers smiles. You’ll not only get to see your work make a difference, you’ll be part of a team working together to help Australians eat and live better every day.

About the team

Strategy is making the future of retail a reality. We’re a big business with big ideas. Our team works together with all our business units to help bring those ideas to life. We stay focused on what’s next, so we can make our customers’ experiences even better now and lead the industry for years to come.

About the role


In this role, you will partner closely with the Head of Group Strategy and GM Corporate Strategy, Business Development & Capital to design and deliver Coles’ long‑term strategic agenda. You will play a critical role in shaping enterprise priorities, supporting executive decision‑making, and delivering high‑impact strategic initiatives across the Group.


You will empower and guide others to generate hypotheses, conduct robust data analysis, synthesise and interpret key themes and trends, and develop structured, evidence‑based solutions to complex business problems. Your key responsibilities will include:



Provide fact‑based insight to assist the development and delivery of key finance and enterprise strategy initiatives.
Lead the development and execution of project plans aligned to the Group’s strategic priorities, providing clear direction to the team.
Drive the delivery and alignment of the Group’s corporate plan.
Lead analysis and structured problem‑solving to develop and articulate initiatives, recommendations, and success metrics on priority strategic issues.
Identify key risks across in‑scope projects and proactively develop mitigation strategies.
Work collaboratively with senior stakeholders as a strategic thought partner, driving alignment and execution of enterprise strategic initiatives.
Guide and support the team in applying effective, efficient analytical methods while ensuring accuracy and quality of analysis.
Facilitate workshops and strategic discussions to advance problem solving, build buy‑in, and achieve agreed outcomes.

About you and your skills


You are an exceptional strategic thinker with demonstrated experience solving complex problems, shaping strategic recommendations, and delivering high‑quality analysis. You bring strong leadership capabilities and can inspire and develop a high‑performing team.


You’ll also have:


Experience in top‑tier management consulting (MBB strongly preferred) or a Corporate/Retail Strategy function within a large, complex organisation.
Experience in writing or contributing to board-level presentations and reports.
Proven experience managing people and delivering projects end‑to‑end.
Deep capability in analytical problem solving, financial analysis and building/working with financial models.
High proficiency using advanced analytical tools and structured problem‑solving techniques.

What’s in it for you?

Flexible working options: We know that work is only one part of your life, so we actively encourage a positive work-life balance and provide hybrid working options to help you achieve it.
Office perks: Take advantage of our gym facility and fitness classes, free parking, BBQ area, mini-Coles supermarket, fooderie hub where you can sample new products before they hit the shelves, school holiday program and so much more when you come in.
Discounts: Eligible team members receive 5% discount all year round on your Supermarket and Liquor online and in-store purchases. We also offer additional periods of double discount (10%) at various times throughout the year. As well as hotdeals exclusive to team members that translate into additional savings.
Reward through recognition: Give and receive recognition, linked to our Coles values, through our digital recognition platform ‘mythanks’. You can accumulate points to redeem in the online shop for exciting gifts and electronic gift cards from an extensive range of retailers.
Opportunities for learning and development: No matter where you start within our diverse business, you’ll have experiences, exposure, and education to satisfy you. Discover and explore a variety of career development programs and job-specific training.
Paid parental leave: We understand how important your life outside work is and offer permanent team members paid parental leave to support you in balancing work and family.
Investment in your future: Our annual team member share plan offer allows eligible team members to make regular pre-tax salary sacrifice deductions to purchase Coles Group shares.

About the recruitment process

We’re continuing to build a gender equitable team, and a culture that is just as diverse, inclusive and welcoming as the communities we serve. We are committed to creating a workplace that is safe and respectful for our team. We encourage applications from people of all ages, cultures (including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples), abilities, sexual orientation and gender identities.

We’re happy to adjust our recruitment process to support candidates with disability. For further information and additional contact details visit the ‘Our Recruitment Process’ section of our careers site or email inclusionrecruitment@coles.com.au

This Role Will Challenge You. That’s the Point.

This role is for someone who defines the standard for marketing science not someone who simply executes it.

Forethought is seeking a Director Analytics & Quantitative Solutions to act as the methodological authority for our firm. You will shape how analytical work is designed, evaluated and defended, ensuring the insights guiding major marketing and business decisions are genuinely robust.

Forethought is a 30-year success story with offices in Melbourne and New York, and Australia’s award-winning customer and marketing analytics consultancy. Our clients trust us because we tell them the truth about what is working and what is not.

This role sits at the intersection of analytical rigour and strategic influence, reporting directly to the CEO.

Our Analytical Culture

Forethought has an unusually strong analytical culture for a consultancy of our size.

Across our 40-person team we employ several PhD-qualified researchers spanning marketing science, data science, behavioural science and cultural anthropology. Many of our team combine academic training with deep consulting experience.

Our reputation is built on analytical credibility, intellectual honesty and clear thinking.

The Role

This is not a delivery role. You will not be running trackers or building models day-to-day.

Instead, you will be responsible for how marketing science is practiced across the firm. Defining methodological standards, challenging assumptions and ensuring analytical work stands up to scrutiny from both clients and our own teams.

Key responsibilities include:

Defining and evolving best-practice marketing science methods and measurement frameworks

Acting as the final methodological authority on analytical approaches and outputs

Reviewing and validating complex work across brand measurement, attribution, causal inference and econometric modelling

Building analytical capability through training and mentoring

Safeguarding analytical integrity in complex or high-stakes client situations

Contributing to marketing science-led client offers, intellectual property and thought leadership

Engaging with CMOs, CFOs and senior client stakeholders to explain and defend analytical work

While there are no direct reports, the quality of marketing science at Forethought will ultimately sit with you.

The Work We Do

Our work spans:

Marketing mix modelling and econometric analysis

Causal inference and experimental design

Brand and communications effectiveness

Attribution and cross-channel measurement

Customer and growth analytics

We work closely with senior marketing and commercial leaders to translate rigorous analysis into clear, defensible decisions.

What You Will Bring

PhD preferred (Masters minimum) in Marketing, Statistics, Econometrics, Data Science or related discipline

10+ years’ experience in marketing science, advanced analytics or market research

Deep expertise in brand measurement, attribution, econometrics and causal inference

Strong understanding of experimental design and statistical modelling

Experience in a consulting, advisory or applied analytics environment

Confidence to challenge clients, colleagues or methodologies when required

Ability to explain complex analytical ideas to senior commercial stakeholders

We are particularly interested in candidates who have contributed to thought leadership, including publications or methodological contributions.

What Makes Forethought Distinctive

Direct influence on how marketing science is practiced across the firm

A highly analytical culture

Exposure to senior decision-makers at leading brands

Hybrid working from our Melbourne HQ

Summer Fridays (3pm finish)

Birthday leave

Career development that supports intellectual leadership

Job Title: Data Architect / Tech Lead - Contract - Sydney
Ad Text:
We’re seeking an experienced Data Architect / Tech Lead to join a brand new role working with one of our enterprise clients in the BFSI domain. 

This is a 12 month contract role initially with a view to extend on a 2 year program with the opportunity to also consider a permanent position. 
The role offers hybrid working with 3 days in the office in Sydney CBD close to public transport.              
           
Key Responsibilities:

Provide data architecture at enterprise level for all finance systems including GL, AR, budgets and forecasting.
Play a critical role across the enterprise platform in data and how it will integrate across the various platforms to provide the best outcomes.
Ensuring that the data is integrated and can be sourced easily from various platforms for analysis on external forecasts, products and pricing.
 Drive efficiency by leveraging AI and automation across the platforms.
  Requirements:

 Data Architect / Lead with strong experience working in enterprise financial industry across complex financial platforms.
8-10 years with proven experience working with big data and implementing data solutions. 
Strong finance knowledge across platforms including GL (general ledger), ERPs, Oracle Fusion, TM1 or Anaplan and how they integrate is essential for this role.
Good understanding of AI and automation and how to optimize systems. 
Strong experience in working with senior stakeholders across the business and technology. 
You must have full working rights in Australia to apply for this role and be based in Sydney.

Your new team

Be part of a specialist internal product team dedicated to making development and the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC) efficient, secure, and robust. You will engage with delivery teams, architects, and technical leads to identify opportunities to implement class leading AI solutions and workflows to improve delivery velocity and quality.

As a key member of a technical delivery team, the Business Analyst works closely with the Product Owner and Engineering leads to ensure business and technical requirements are documented clearly. You will serve as a bridge between complex system constraints and modern development needs, ensuring that our internal engineering and delivery "customers" have the AI tools and processes they need to build great features.

The role

SDLC Re-imagination & Discovery: Collaborate with the AI PO to conduct strategic analysis of the end-to-end development lifecycle to identify friction points where AI paradigms and tools can fundamentally replace or accelerate traditional administrative and delivery tasks.
Experimentation & Prototyping (R&D): Collaborate with the AI team to execute rapid, scientific experiments with emerging AI tools to validate hypotheses, ensuring a "fail fast" approach to innovation before scaling successful solutions to the wider business.
Workflow & Guardrail Definition: Collaborate with the AI team to design and document "AI-ready" workflows that integrate strict guardrails and  governance to ensure generated outputs meet quality, security, and compliance standards.
Change Management & Adoption: Champion the cultural adoption of AI within engineering teams by showcasing tangible value, gathering user feedback, and actively addressing resistance to new ways of working.
Requirement Definition: Translate complex R&D concepts into clear technical specifications for the AI engineering team, balancing the capabilities of off-the-shelf tools (e.g., Copilot) with custom built-in-house solutions.
Benefits Realisation: Quantify the success of AI initiatives by establishing performance baselines and tracking key metrics such as velocity, bug reduction, and cycle time improvements.

What experience you’ll bring

Essential Experience

Business Analysis: Minimum of 3 years’ experience as a Business Analyst, preferably within a technical delivery, platform, or R&D team.
SDLC Expertise: Deep understanding of software delivery methodologies (Agile, Scrum, Waterfall, DevOps). You must know the "rules" intimately in order to know how to break and rewrite them.
AI Literacy: Demonstrated interest in Generative AI tools. You must understand the practical limitations of current models (e.g., hallucinations, context windows, non-deterministic outcomes) to define realistic requirements and workflows.
Process documentation: Ability to map complex workflows and visualize how data and decisions flow between humans and systems.
Experience in a regulated environment (preferably Insurance/Finance) where Data restrictions and information security are paramount.

Skills & Capabilities

Experimental Mindset: Comfortable working with ambiguity. You view failure in an experiment as a data point, not a setback.
Technical Logic: While you do not need to code, you must be able to follow technical logic, understand API integrations, and converse fluently with developers about architectural constraints.
Stakeholder Influence: Ability to facilitate workshops with highly technical audiences and drive consensus on new, unproven ways of working.
Problem Solving: Strong analytical skills to deconstruct systemic inefficiencies and propose innovative solutions.

Desirable (Beneficial)

Advanced AI Concepts: Familiarity with concepts such as RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation), MCPs (Model Context Protocol), multi-agent architectures, prompt engineering and token limits.
AI Tooling: Experience with or exposure to specific platforms like AWS Bedrock and GitHub Copilot.
Experience with developer tooling (Jira, GitHub/GitLab, CI/CD pipelines).

Location

Close to a variety of public transport and easy parking options, the Toowong office has excellent end of trip facilities (including secure bike storage and showers), outdoor spaces and plenty of modern collaborative areas to work and connect with colleagues.

Conveniently based at Toowong Village tower with views of the mountains and the Brisbane River, our location is only 4 km from the city and provides access to retail, restaurants and other amenities.

The benefits of working at A&G

Be rewarded - we recognise high performance and reward our people for their hard work through bonuses and other perks.
Work flexibility - with options to work from home two days per week.
Options for leave - life happens, so we’ve got volunteer days, an additional paid ‘ME’ day, paid parental leave and the opportunity to purchase additional leave to cover all of the big stuff.
Grow with us - we’ve got learning and professional development opportunities to suit everyone.
Give back - our A&G Difference program gives you the power to change our community for the better through volunteering, fundraising and donation opportunities for causes that you’re passionate about.

Ability Roundtable Lead Data Engineer

 

Employment: Permanent, full-time

Location: Hybrid/remote with some travel




The Opportunity

We’re hiring a Lead Data Engineer to own and evolve the data platform behind Australia’s leading disability sector benchmarking service. You’ll be the most senior technical person on the platform, responsible for its architecture, reliability, and future direction.

 We collect financial and operational data from disability and care providers, transform it using standardised business rules, and deliver benchmarking insights that help these organisations improve outcomes for people with disabilities. The platform runs on Microsoft Fabric and processes structured data submissions across multiple benchmarking programs.

This role sits at a critical point in our journey. We are investing in our data capability and laying foundations for the future of disability and care sector benchmarking.  This is a newly created role with real architectural authority and genuine autonomy. You’ll shape technical decisions and work closely with our Data Scientist to build the layer between raw submissions and trusted analytics. And you’ll do it in a sector where the data directly influences how support is delivered to some of Australia’s most vulnerable communities.

You’ll be joining a value-driven organisation that believes great work happens when people are trusted, supported, and given the flexibility to balance life and career. We offer genuinely flexible working arrangements, strong employee benefits, and a supportive environment where people can do meaningful work.

 

We are seeking a Lead Data Engineer with a minimum 5 years’ demonstrated experience working in a data platform management role with experience designing and building data pipelines and ingestion workflows, strong SQL proficiency, experience with data lakehouse and/ or data warehouse architecture and high attention to detail.

If you’re motivated by purpose, enjoy solving meaningful problems, and want to build something enduring in a collaborative, flexible workplace — this role offers the chance to do exactly that.

What you’ll do

 This is an opportunity for a Lead Data Engineer who wants more than just a technical role — someone who is motivated by purpose, enjoys ownership, and wants to help shape something important in the disability sector.

As the technical authority for our data platform, the Lead Data Engineer takes end-to-end responsibility for its stewardship and evolution into the future. This includes platform architecture, security, governance, and ensuring that sensitive data is managed in line with privacy legislation, contractual obligations, and the trust placed in us by member organisations.

 A core focus of the role is building and maintaining data quality and trust. You will define and document data standards, establish best practices, and embed quality controls throughout the data lifecycle — ensuring the platform continues to support reliable benchmarking and meaningful insight.

 A core responsibility of the role is the design and management of the data ingestion layer, including the creation and maintenance of pipelines that process structured flat files in preparation for downstream reporting and analysis. The role also oversees the ongoing development of validation tools to assess data quality at the point of ingestion, ensuring issues are identified and resolved early.

 The Lead Data Engineer owns the storage and curated layers of the platform with the data lake sitting at the centre of the architecture as the primary data environment. This role also encompasses the planned introduction of a data warehouse to support structured reporting needs over time, ensuring both environments remain well-organised, efficient, and scalable as the organisation's needs change.

 Working closely with the Data Scientist, the Data Platform Lead designs and manages the semantic layer that underpins reporting and analysis. This involves defining business metrics, hierarchies, and relationships in a consistent and governed way. The role is responsible for the ongoing performance and reliability of the semantic layer, ensuring it stays aligned with evolving business definitions and remains a dependable source of truth.

 

Key Accountabilities:

Under delegation from, and/or working alongside, the General Manager and Data Scientist, the role will be accountable for:

 

Platform Management and Governance

·        Serve as the technical authority for the Microsoft Fabric data platform, including its overall management, security, and evolution.

·        Ensure complex and sensitive data is handled in accordance with contracts, formal agreements, membership terms, and privacy legislation.

·        Support leadership in identifying and managing platform-level risks, providing technical insight to inform decision-making and ensure appropriate controls are in place.

·        Monitor and maintain platform performance, ensuring the platform remains responsive and reliable for all users.

Data Quality & Standards

·        Define and maintain data standards, best practices, and business rules that form the basis for identifying and resolving data errors.

·        Document and maintain resources such as data dictionaries and pipeline flow diagrams.

·        Investigate and resolve data quality issues as they arise.

·        Develop and maintain validation tools to assess data quality at the point of ingestion, ensuring issues are identified and resolved early.

Data Architecture & Pipeline Engineering

·        Design, build, and maintain data ingestion pipelines that process structured flat files in preparation for downstream reporting and analysis.

·        Design and maintain the organisation's data lake as the primary data environment, with a view to introducing a data warehouse over time.

·        Work closely with the Data Scientist to design and manage the semantic layer, ensuring business metrics, hierarchies, and relationships are defined consistently.

·        Maintain version control across platform code and configuration, ensuring changes are tracked, reversible, and clearly documented.

·        Work with data submitters to manage and improve submission consistency ahead of full automated validation.

·        Continuously identify and implement improvements to the platform, keeping it efficient and aligned with the organisation's changing needs.

What we offer

A competitive salary, commensurate with experience for a lead-level data engineering role in the Australian market. You will also receive superannuation and not-for-profit salary packaging benefits including pre-tax eligible expenses, meal and entertainment benefits, and FBT covered by the organisation. Mobile and internet costs are reimbursed.

 Flexible hybrid/remote working arrangements. A small, collaborative team where your work has visible impact. The chance to shape a growing platform from the ground up, and to do technically meaningful work that improves outcomes for people with disability across Australia.

 

What you bring

Essential

•        Tertiary qualifications in Computer Science, Information Technology, Data Engineering, or a related field.

•        5+ years’ experience designing and operating data platforms or pipelines in a production environment.

•        Hands-on experience with Microsoft Fabric, or strong skills in a comparable data platform (Databricks, Synapse, BigQuery, Snowflake) with willingness to work in Fabric.

•        Demonstrated ability to design data lakehouse or warehouse architectures, not just maintain existing ones.

•        Strong Python and SQL proficiency. These are your daily tools.

•        Experience building data quality frameworks or validation tooling, not just running data checks, but designing the approach.

•        Working knowledge of data governance, privacy obligations, and ethical data handling, particularly with sensitive or multi-party data.

•        Comfortable operating as the sole technical owner of a platform. You can prioritise, make architectural calls, and explain trade-offs to non-technical stakeholders.

•        Familiarity with Power BI or equivalent BI tools.

•        Willingness to complete pre-employment checks including Worker Screening.

Desirable

•        Experience in the disability, health, aged care, or human services sector.

•        Understanding of the NDIS regulatory and reform environment.

•        Experience working with complex, multi-organisational datasets where data quality varies across contributors.

•        Experience supporting regulatory or sector-level reporting for external stakeholders.

•        Proficiency in Scala, Spark, R, or other languages used in data/analytics contexts.




About the Ability Roundtable

 The Ability Roundtable is a disability sector benchmarking service that supports service improvement through data, peer learning, and collaboration. It provides participating organisations with the opportunity to benchmark their services across Australia through a confidential and trusted platform. Benchmarking helps organisations identify best practices, track performance, and improve the quality of the support they provide.

 The Ability Roundtable currently hosts several well-established benchmarking programs across a range of service areas, including Financial & Workforce, Home & Living, and Allied Health. In recent years, it has expanded its scope to include benchmarking in Quality & Safety and Supported Employment. Each program collects structured data from participating organisations on a regular cycle and applies standardised definitions and business rules to ensure consistency and comparability. The data feeds into the Ability Roundtable’s analytics platform to enable peer benchmarking and interactive reporting.

 The Ability Roundtable’s benchmarking activities are enabled by a purpose-built platform designed to collect, validate, analyse, and present benchmarking data at scale.

As we continue to evolve our digital ecosystem, we're investing in new ways to support the wellbeing of the people and communities we serve.



We're looking for a Senior Business Analyst who's passionate about creating meaningful digital experiences and is ready to play a key role in uplifting our wellbeing offerings across member‑facing platforms.



This is an initial 6-month Contract with a day rate of $900 incl super per day.

Your impact



You'll help shape and deliver a series of digital wellbeing initiatives designed to enhance how our members access support, navigate services, and engage with wellbeing resources.

This is a role for someone who genuinely thrives in collaborative environments and is motivated by improving health outcomes for Australians.

What you'll be doing



Leading discovery activities to understand current member journeys and wellbeing pathways
Translating insights into clear, actionable requirements that elevate digital experiences
Supporting integrations with internal platforms and third‑party partners
Defining analytics and reporting needs to help us better understand member behaviour and improve services
Producing high‑quality BA artefacts, process maps, user stories, and documentation
Facilitating workshops to bring together diverse stakeholders and align on priorities
Ensuring requirements remain traceable end‑to‑end through design, build, and testing
Collaborating across multiple squads to keep delivery smooth, transparent, and connected

What you bring

A strong communicator who builds trust quickly and adapts across audiences
Naturally proactive — you take ownership, bring clarity, and help teams move forward
Detail‑oriented, while always keeping an eye on the bigger picture and member outcomes

Why Medibank

Senior Actuary - NSW Government Department


Payrate: $1087 per day + Super
Location: McKell Building, Sydney or Gosford
Contract until: 31/03/2027 - POTENTIAL extensions



Key Responsibilities

Support the Chief Actuary in developing actuarial strategy and establishing internal operating frameworks, governance, and best practices
Lead and mentor a team of actuarial professionals, fostering capability development and a strong professional culture
Deliver high-quality actuarial analysis and insights to support decision-making across a diverse stakeholder group
Communicate complex actuarial concepts and modelling results clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences
Lead scheme monitoring and forecasting to assess sustainability, performance drivers, and emerging risks
Provide actuarial input across pricing, reserving, and regulatory reporting
Conduct and lead peer reviews of actuarial work produced internally and by regulated insurers
Essential Requirement

Tertiary qualification in Actuarial Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field
Proven experience leading actuarial analysts or teams
Minimum 7 years’ actuarial experience, including extensive general insurance experience

About the Company

This organisation is a well-established leader in the utilities sector, delivering critical services to a large and growing customer base. With a strong focus on innovation, sustainability, and future-ready technology, they are investing heavily in data, AI, and advanced analytics.


Their technology function is undergoing a major uplift, with AI positioned as a key driver of smarter decision-making, operational efficiency, and long-term value creation. You'll be joining a business where AI is not just experimental-it's becoming embedded across the enterprise.

About the Role

This is a senior, hands-on leadership role responsible for building and scaling enterprise AI capability. As Lead Data Engineer, you'll design, develop, and deploy AI and machine learning solutions that deliver tangible business outcomes across complex operational environments.




You'll work closely with data leaders, architects, and business stakeholders to identify opportunities and bring high-impact AI use cases to life-from predictive models through to cutting-edge generative AI solutions.


Key responsibilities include:



Leading end-to-end AI/ML solution design, development, and deployment
Driving innovation across NLP, LLMs, and advanced ML techniques
Establishing and scaling MLOps practices (CI/CD, monitoring, lifecycle management)
Building on modern cloud and data platforms (Azure, Databricks)
Embedding AI into enterprise systems to improve efficiency and decision-making
Championing governance, best practice, and responsible AI frameworks
Mentoring engineers and leading cross-functional delivery teams

This role offers a strong mix of strategy and hands-on engineering, with real influence over how AI is adopted across the organisation.

About You

You're an experienced AI/ML engineer with a strong track record of delivering production-ready solutions at scale. You're comfortable operating across both technical and business domains and enjoy turning complex ideas into practical outcomes.


You'll bring:


Strong background in software or data engineering (Python preferred)
Experience with cloud platforms (Azure preferred) and Databricks ecosystem
Deep experience with ML frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit-learn)
Solid MLOps capability, including model deployment and monitoring
Exposure to generative AI, LLMs, and modern AI frameworks (e.g. LangChain)
Proven ability to deliver AI solutions that drive measurable business value
Strong stakeholder engagement and leadership experience
